articles.title: Yemen's Houthis claim Red Sea tanker strikes, as Trump threatens Iran's power plants
articles.description: The escalations intensify the risk of further pressure on global oil supply and prices.

related.title: Trump's new Iran threat
related.description: Donald Trump has issued a new threat to destroy a bridge or power plant every time Iran attacks shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Insect repellent release leaves 11 staff sick at major SA hospital
articles.description: Eleven staff at an Adelaide hospital have become unwell and a dozen emergency beds have been closed, after the accidental release of an "outdoor insect repellent" inside the facility.

articles.title: Trump went to war to restrict a nuclear program, now he's backing one
articles.description: Donald Trump went to war with Tehran to stop it enriching nuclear fuel. Now he's approved a deal that analysts warn could open the door for another Middle Eastern power to potentially do exactly that.

articles.title: Driver boasted of drink driving, weeks before killing 12-year-old girl
articles.description: County Court judge Michael Tinney says Jordan Kenyon's decision to get behind the wheel after consuming the drug GHB was like playing "Russian roulette" with the general public.

articles.title: ASX trims gains as jobs data stokes rate hike bets; miners, James Hardie rise
articles.description: The Australian sharemarket pared most of its early gains after the nation’s latest job market figures came in stronger than expected, keeping the chances of another interest rate rise next month on the table.

articles.title: ‘I want to kill her’: Jail for horrific strangling of woman on a Perth street caught on CCTV
articles.description: Warning: This story contains violent footage that some readers may find distressing. The 29-year-old FIFO worker was charged with attempted murder after he strangled the woman following months of stalking, harassment and abuse.

articles.description: A military shake-up has eased the unrest for now, but revealed the political strains building as the war against Russia drags on.

articles.title: Money to burn? Big Tech’s AI splurge is blowing out
articles.description: The market reaction to the results of Google’s parent Alphabet and Elon Musk’s Tesla suggests that investors are becoming increasingly wary of the tech giants’ ballooning spending on AI.

articles.title: A nuclear-sized risk? The US-Saudi deal changing the equation in the Middle East
articles.description: A landmark agreement has revived longstanding concerns, and raised fresh questions about the Middle East's nuclear future.

articles.title: Taiwanese woman sentenced for attempted murder of Australian exchange student
articles.description: Taiwanese media has reported Yang Jinping has been sentenced for the attempted murder of Brisbane exchange student Alex Shorey in 2023.

articles.title: Road to nowhere blocked by Indigenous heritage claim resolved after four years
articles.description: One of Western Australia's longest bridges will be built to fix a road to nowhere in Perth's east — resolving a four-year long deadlock over fears the bridge would destroy an ancient rock shelter and billabong.

articles.title: Bystanders hailed as 'heroes' after stopping attempted murder on CCTV
articles.description: A man who "doggedly" tried to kill a woman in a prolonged attack in suburban Perth caught on CCTV has is been jailed for 16 years.

articles.title: Court hears complainant's evidence challenged in police officer's rape trial
articles.description: The defence team of a former NSW Police officer accused of raping a 17-year-old girl in 1998 has told the court that the complainant's claims are unreliable.

articles.title: Argument over dog leads to shooting murder and 22 years' jail
articles.description: The family of a man who was fatally shot through a screen door during an argument over a dog say the sentence handed to his killer is "bittersweet".

articles.title: Sovereign citizen question to gun owners shut down by senior officer
articles.description: A senior police officer slams the strategy of writing to some Tasmanian gun owners asking them to confirm or deny whether they aligned with sovereign citizen ideology.

articles.title: Macquarie chief to walk away with hundreds of millions of dollars
articles.description: The chief of the “millionaires’ factory” will depart in November with a significant personal treasure chest built up over nearly four decades with the company.

articles.title: 'Stop talking about it': Joey shuts door on coaching
articles.description: Andrew Johns firmly shuts the door on coaching the NSW Blues and says it won't happen, despite loud calls for him to take the job.

articles.title: Elite boarding school review uncovers 'normalised' abuse
articles.description: An independent review of an elite private school in regional Victoria describes a culture of "normalised" racism, bullying and abuse.

articles.title: CCTV vision captures good Samaritans stepping in to stop violent attack
articles.description: Mihael Vrhovsek waited outside a woman's apartment before violently attacking her, with bystanders stepping in to stop his attack. Footage released by WA courts.

articles.title: This is how the Hawks could pull off a heist for the ages
articles.description: Hawthorn are willing to risk losing young guns for the upside of what Ben King could deliver, but would love to keep them all and bring in Zach Merrett, too – at the right price.

articles.title: Man charged after Malvern East rampage, chainsaw siege
articles.description: A quiet residential estate was crawling with heavily armed officers after a 43-year-old allegedly threatened police with a chainsaw.

articles.title: Outback hubs prepare for 'amazingly majestic' 2028 solar eclipse
articles.description: About 10,000 eclipse chasers are expected to descend on the Northern Territory's Barkly region in two years to stand in the shadow of the moon.

articles.title: Former MP convicted of three extra counts of deception
articles.description: Former SA state MP Fraser Ellis now stands convicted of seven counts of deception after failing to have his original convictions thrown out on appeal, and having several acquittals overturned.

articles.title: Save our town: Lancelin residents take coastal erosion fight to city centre
articles.description: With 50 metres of coast washed away in a year, Lancelin residents are rallying in Perth on Thursday to demand state intervention before the ocean breaks through.

articles.title: Gold Coast CEO's resignation blamed on 'unrelenting personal attacks'
articles.description: A veteran Gold Coast councillor claims chief executive Tim Baker's resignation followed a "deliberate campaign of unrelenting personal attacks", describing his departure as "a very bad outcome" for the city.

articles.description: Love it or hate it, the film has sparked a lot of discussion. Here, we bring some of it together.

articles.title: ‘Makes me sick to my stomach’: Students speak out about strapping scandal

articles.title: Most wanted man won’t be extradited from Greece over fatal Sydney stabbing: reports
articles.description: Greece has ruled out sending James Dalamangas back to Sydney to face a court, decades after a fatal nightclub stabbing.

articles.title: Bridal shop employee faces jail time for defrauding dozens of brides, court hears
articles.description: The 53-year-old wanted to plead guilty to a fraud charge but a magistrate has ruled the case must be referred to a higher court with greater sentencing powers.

articles.title: Sydney man fined for wearing Nazi symbol says he's 'trashed' his life
articles.description: A man who wore a T-shirt with a banned Nazi symbol outside the antisemitism royal commission in Sydney has been convicted and fined $1,500.

articles.title: 'I don't want a circus': ACT Education Minister resigns from cabinet
articles.description: ACT Education Minister Yvette Berry resigns after the Integrity Commission made "serious corrupt conduct" findings against two senior public servants in her portfolio.

articles.title: ‘I failed as a parent’: Lesli’s children didn’t feel safe in their own home during Bondi attack
articles.description: A father fought back tears at the antisemitism royal commission as he recounted how his children had feared for their lives in their own home.

articles.title: 'Bit angry': Confusion after Crows star's ban
articles.description: Rory Sloane admits his confusion after Adelaide star Darcy Fogarty was slapped with a three-game ban.

articles.title: Plastic, by Charmonix Vaatuitui
articles.description: Sixteen-year-old western Sydney high school Charmonix Vaatuitui, made a short film about her Samoan and Tongan heritage, as part of a week-long creative course run by the University of Technology.
